name: Corn with Barbecue Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and minced garlic; fry for 2 to 3 minutes until fragrant.
2. Stir in the chopped tomatoes, diced green bell pepper, diced green chilies, grated ginger, tomato paste, malt vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, vegetable stock, muscovado sugar, and salt. Bring the mixture to a boil, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes with the lid off.
3. Remove the lid for the last 10 minutes of cooking. Allow the sauce to cool slightly.
4. Puree the sauce by pressing it through a fine sieve or blender until smooth.
5. Prepare the corn: Slice the kernels from the cobs and discard any leaves and silk threads. Place the corn in a large pot of boiling, salted water and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until tender (Note: Some may find this cooking time longer than necessary).
6. Drain the corn.
7. Return the sauce to the pan over medium heat; bring it back to a boil. Add the dry sherry and cook for an additional couple of minutes.
8. Spoon the hot sauce over the corn and serve immediately.
